input.prompt:
I'm building a photo viewer page in my .NET MAUI app targeting .NET 10. I want
the image to fill the entire screen edge-to-edge, going behind the status bar
and navigation bar. But my image still has padding around it even though I set
SafeAreaEdges="None" on the ContentPage. What am I missing?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a photo viewer page in my .NET MAUI app targeting .NET 10. I want
the image to fill the entire screen edge-to-edge, going behind the status bar
and navigation bar. But my image still has padding around it even though I set
SafeAreaEdges="None" on the ContentPage. What am I missing?

data.content:
`SafeAreaEdges` isn't a built-in .NET MAUI property — that's likely why it has no effect. Here's what you actually need:

### 1. iOS — disable safe area insets on the page

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns:ios="clr-namespace:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.PlatformConfiguration.iOSSpecific;assembly=Microsoft.Maui.Controls"
             ios:Page.UseSafeArea="False">
```

### 2. Hide the app's navigation/tab bars

```xml
<ContentPage ...
             Shell.NavBarIsVisible="False"
             Shell.TabBarIsVisible="False">
```

### 3. Use a Grid (not StackLayout) with an AspectFill image

```xml
<Grid>
    <Image Source="photo.jpg" Aspect="AspectFill" />
</Grid>
```

### 4. Android — draw behind system bars

On Android 15+ edge-to-edge is automatic, but on older versions you need to opt in. In your `Platforms/Android/MainActivity.cs`:

```csharp
protected override void OnCreate(Bundle? savedInstanceState)
{
    base.OnCreate(savedInstanceState);

    if (OperatingSystem.IsAndroidVersionAtLeast(30))
    {
        Window?.SetDecorFitsSystemWindows(false);
    }
    else
    {
        // For Android < 30
        Window?.SetFlags(
            Android.Views.WindowManagerFlags.LayoutNoLimits,
            Android.Views.WindowManagerFlags.LayoutNoLimits);
    }
}
```

### Full XAML example

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             xmlns:ios="clr-namespace:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.PlatformConfiguration.iOSSpecific;assembly=Microsoft.Maui.Controls"
             ios:Page.UseSafeArea="False"
             Shell.NavBarIsVisible="False"
             Shell.TabBarIsVisible="False"
             x:Class="MyApp.PhotoViewerPage">
    <Grid>
        <Image Source="photo.jpg" Aspect="AspectFill" />
    </Grid>
</ContentPage>
```

**TL;DR:** Replace `SafeAreaEdges="None"` with `ios:Page.UseSafeArea="False"`, hide nav/tab bars, and opt into edge-to-edge on Android via `SetDecorFitsSystemWindows(false)`.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about making content go edge-to-edge in a .NET MAUI app, going behind the status bar and navigation bar. They've tried `SafeAreaEdges="None"` on the ContentPage but still see padding.

This is a common .NET MAUI question. Let me think about what's needed:

In .NET MAUI, to achieve true edge-to-edge content:

1. `IgnoreSafeArea="True"` on the layout/view itself is important
2. On iOS, you need to set `Page.IgnoreSafeArea` or use platform-specific APIs


3. On Android, edge-to-edge is now the default in newer Android versions, but you may need to handle it explicitly

I'm realizing `SafeAreaEdges` isn't actually a standard MAUI property—the key is `IgnoreSafeArea` on the view itself, not the page. On iOS, safe area insets push content inward by default, so you need `IgnoreSafeArea="True"` on whichever view should extend behind the bars. Let me reconsider the approach here.

For .NET MAUI, the iOS-specific way is `ios:Page.UseSafeArea="False"` or calling `SetUseSafeArea(false)` in code. On Android, edge-to-edge behavior depends on the API level—Android 15 enforces it, but earlier versions need `Window.SetDecorFitsSystemWindows(false)` or the newer edge-to-edge APIs. The view itself may need additional configuration depending on what you're trying to achieve.

The user mentioned `SafeAreaEdges="None"`, which isn't a standard MAUI API—they might be mixing up terminology. To actually go edge-to-edge in MAUI, I'd use the platform-specific properties on the page level for iOS and configure the window behavior on Android.

Setting up the Image element to extend edge-to-edge... I need to clarify the right approach for .NET MAUI - `SafeAreaEdges` isn't the solution here, and I should focus on disabling safe area handling with `ios:Page.UseSafeArea="False"` on iOS, hiding navigation elements with `Shell.NavBarIsVisible="False"`, and ensuring the Image uses `AspectFill` in a Grid layout. On Android, I'll need to handle the window drawing behind system bars as well.
